execution_boundary.summary: PROV represents provenance relationships and does not execute actions.

preparation_boundary.summary: Entity, activity, and agent relations can prepare provenance evidence.

commitment_boundary.summary: Provenance may inform commitment review but cannot authorize it.

semantic_equivalence_boundary.summary: Alternate provenance encodings require preserved relationship meaning.

unknown_trajectory_boundary.summary: Incomplete or unresolved provenance requires explicit uncertainty posture.

authority_boundary.summary: Agent association or attribution is not execution authority.

evidence_freshness_boundary.summary: Provenance timing and superseding records matter at commit time.

reconstruction_boundary.summary: Entities, activities, agents, relations, identifiers, and timing support reconstruction.

interoperability_path.summary: PROV graphs can enter the evidence layer through a provenance adapter.

next_required_action: add complete-graph, missing-relation, superseded-entity, and alternate-encoding fixtures
